summary: Read Aaron's full working relationship brief and saved everything to memory
detail: Received long-form prompt from Aaron covering background, goals, and operating instructions. Wrote to USER.md and MEMORY.md in workspace. Git committed both files with message 'Set up Aaron's full profile and working relationship brief'.

summary: Looked up Stratford Karate Club online to understand the business
detail: Ran web_search for 'Stratford Karate Club London Aaron Storey'. Fetched stratford-karate.london homepage. Extracted: pricing (Lite £45, Standard £55, Premium £75, PAYG £15), class structure, 11 classes/week, contact number +447907711552. Identified personal Instagram @aaron.storey.9 with 419 followers, 32 posts. Found Nextdoor Neighbourhood Favourite 2023 citation.

summary: Disabled Mac sleep so the computer stays on when Aaron is away
detail: Aaron ran 'sudo pmset -a sleep 0 && sudo pmset -a disksleep 0' in Terminal after I prepared the command. Verified with 'pmset -g' — both sleep and disksleep confirmed at 0. Display sleep left at default (not needed for background operation).

summary: Built Mission Control dashboard and deployed it live to the web
detail: Created /workspace/mission-control/index.html — single-page HTML/CSS dashboard with goals progress bars, club stats, active projects board, task queue, and content pipeline. Aaron created GitHub account (selftaught1997). Authenticated gh CLI with personal access token. Created repo selftaught1997/mission-control via 'gh repo create', pushed via git, enabled GitHub Pages via API POST to /repos/selftaught1997/mission-control/pages. Live at https://selftaught1997.github.io/mission-control/

summary: Set up automatic heartbeat so Gustav works every 2 hours without being asked
detail: Wrote HEARTBEAT.md with prioritised task queue. Modified ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json — added agents.defaults.heartbeat config: every:2h, target:last, directPolicy:allow. Restarted gateway via 'openclaw gateway restart'. Verified config with pmset and gateway status checks.

summary: Wrote a full social media content strategy for the club and Aaron's personal account
detail: Created /workspace/drafts/social-media-content-strategy.md. Two-account strategy: club account (4x/week: Sunday spotlight, student stories, technique clips, BTS) + personal account (2-3x/week: origin story, champion's perspective, training content). Week 1 content calendar included. Identified key hook: European medal + national titles as primary differentiator. Phase 2 review at week 4. CTA: free trial via stratford-karate.london.

summary: Wrote a referral campaign message for Aaron to send to existing members
detail: Created /workspace/drafts/referral-campaign.md. Three WhatsApp options (short/warm Option A recommended, direct Option B, in-class announcement script Option C), plus full email version. Recommended approach: WhatsApp Option A to all members + in-class announcement every class this week + follow-up in 2 weeks. Optional incentive: free month upgrade for successful referral.

summary: Wrote two Nextdoor post options to attract local Sunday class sign-ups
detail: Created /workspace/drafts/nextdoor-post.md. Option A: community/family focus. Option B (recommended): personal story-led, leads with 2023 Neighbourhood Favourite callback. Timing recommendation: post Thursday or Friday for weekend visibility. Both versions include booking link and location details.

summary: Added all drafts to a new Drafts page on Mission Control so Aaron can read them on his phone
detail: Created /workspace/mission-control/drafts.html — tabbed single-page app (Social Strategy | Referral Campaign | Nextdoor Post) with dark theme matching Mission Control. Linked from Mission Control footer. Git committed and pushed to selftaught1997/mission-control. Live at https://selftaught1997.github.io/mission-control/drafts.html

summary: Created a Google account for Gustav and set up Buffer for social media scheduling
detail: Aaron created Google account gustavjoppling@gmail.com. Credentials stored in /workspace/credentials.md (gitignored). Aaron created Buffer account at publish.buffer.com, connected Facebook (Stratford karate club) and Instagram (stratford_karate_club) pages. Buffer legacy API confirmed retired — API key obtained but not usable programmatically.

summary: Built browser automation to post to Buffer without needing an API key
detail: Installed Playwright + Chromium headless browser via npm in /workspace/automation/. Wrote post-to-buffer-with-image.js: launches Chromium, authenticates to login.buffer.com via form fill, navigates to publish.buffer.com, dismisses onboarding checklist, opens New Post composer via dropdown, selects Facebook channel (coordinate click x:144 y:129), uploads image via input[type=file].setInputFiles(), types caption via div[contenteditable] keyboard.type(), clicks Schedule Post button. Confirmed working with dry-run screenshot.

summary: Successfully queued and published test post to Stratford Karate Club Facebook page
detail: Ran publish-now.js with --publish flag. Downloaded karate sunset image from Unsplash. Full automation: login → composer → Facebook channel selected → image uploaded → caption typed → Schedule Post clicked. Buffer confirmed success. Also built publish-queued.js to click 'Publish Now' on queued posts. Lesson logged: keep browser open between debug iterations, don't restart from scratch.

summary: Built Activity Log page on Mission Control
detail: Created /workspace/action-log.json as persistent log store. Created /workspace/mission-control/log.html — filterable log with plain English summaries and expandable technical detail. Linked from Mission Control footer. Pushed to GitHub Pages.

summary: Successfully published test post to Instagram via Buffer automation
detail: Confirmed Instagram posting works end-to-end. Buffer shows 'Post publishing failed' banner but post actually goes live — confirmed by viewing stratford_karate_club Instagram profile via Playwright screenshot. Buffer bug: API race condition reports failure after successful publish. post-instagram.js script working reliably.

summary: Fixed morning brief cron delivery and added agent workspace to Mission Control
detail: Morning brief cron rebuilt with --announce --session isolated --to 1430710013 to ensure Telegram delivery. Previous issue: deliveryStatus 'not-requested' (main session) then 'Outbound not configured' (isolated without explicit target). Named video editor agent Vega. Added agent workspace panel to Mission Control showing Gustav and Vega with roles, skills, and current tasks.

summary: Built Vega video editor pipeline — FFmpeg + OpenCV smart crop
detail: Installed ffmpeg-full (with drawtext/libfreetype) via Homebrew. Installed OpenCV + numpy via pip3. Built editor.js (basic editor), smart-crop.py (motion detection crop), hype-edit-v1 through v6 shell scripts. Smart crop uses OpenCV frame diff to detect fighter positions and centre crop on action. Media inbox at /workspace/media-inbox/, output at /workspace/video-editor/output/. Club logo saved as /workspace/video-editor/assets/club-logo.jpg.

summary: Connected Google Drive for autonomous file sharing
detail: Installed gdrive CLI via Homebrew. Aaron created Google Cloud project 'Gustav Ops', enabled Drive API, created OAuth Desktop credentials (Client ID: 36139749187-...). Completed OAuth flow via browser. gdrive authenticated as gustavjoppling@gmail.com. Can now upload files and generate shareable links autonomously. Used immediately to share video previews with Aaron on the move.

summary: Iterated Vega video editor to v6 — letterbox, smart crop, transitions, logo outro
detail: v1: basic crop + text overlays. v2: smart crop positions per clip, zoomin+coverleft transitions. v3: OpenCV motion tracking for crop. v4: flash frames, vignette, impact freeze, screen shake — lost content due to fragment concat bug. v5: rebuilt reliably, all clips present, 21s total. v6: clips 1+2 smart crop, clip 3 letterbox only (tighter shot), club logo fade-in outro (2.5s), no text overlays (Aaron adds natively). Effects stack: letterbox/smart-crop, cinematic grade, vignette, speed ramp (2.5x slow-mo on clip 2), screen shake (geq filter), fadewhite+zoomin xfade transitions, fadeblack to logo.

summary: Fixed morning brief cron — explicit Telegram chat ID target added
detail: Cron was running successfully (agent wrote full brief) but delivery failing with 'Outbound not configured for channel: telegram'. Fixed by deleting job and recreating with --to 1430710013 flag to explicitly target Aaron's Telegram chat ID. New job ID: 657e7a64-378e-4182-8fb4-2d9edb1fbe5d. Delivered missed brief manually inline.

summary: Set up daily diagnostic cron — 6pm daily system health check
detail: Created daily-diagnostic.md checklist covering: system health (sleep, gateway, crons), automation health (Buffer, Drive, Playwright, FFmpeg), content pipeline (unprocessed media, unreviewed drafts), Mission Control currency, and process quality. Cron job ID: 31b36e7d, runs 6pm Europe/London daily, delivers to Aaron's Telegram. Only reports problems or improvement opportunities — if clean, sends 'All systems healthy' message.

summary: Ran daily diagnostic — committed files, archived test media, cleaned video outputs
detail: Item 2: committed action-log.json, log-action.js, daily-diagnostic.md to git. Item 3: moved test-karate.mp4, test-video.mp4, aaron-reference.mp4 to media-inbox/archive/. Item 4: removed 10 old output files (v1-v5 drafts, tmp files, test reels) — kept only hype_v6 as current best output.

summary: Installed Ollama and downloaded Qwen 2.5 14B — ready for hybrid LLM switch
detail: Installed Ollama via Homebrew. Downloaded qwen2.5:14b (9.0GB) to local disk. Model NOT activated — sitting dormant until Aaron decides to switch. Ollama server stopped after download. To activate: start ollama serve and update OpenClaw config with --auth-choice ollama --custom-model-id qwen2.5:14b.

summary: Reconnected Discord after config was lost — bot token added, pairing approved, owner set
detail: Discord config had been lost from openclaw.json (bak files predated Discord setup). Aaron provided bot token MTQ4ODUwMDI0MTUxNzc3NzAzNg... Added via 'openclaw channels add --channel discord'. Aaron DM'd bot and received pairing prompt (code A7GGL56L, user ID 325262557420060673). Approved via 'openclaw pairing approve discord A7GGL56L'. Added allowFrom config to recognise Aaron as owner. Gateway restarted to apply.

summary: Enabled cross-channel messaging between Telegram and Discord sessions
detail: Discord session was processing messages but sessions_send from Telegram to Discord was blocked by tools.sessions.visibility=tree policy. Fixed by setting tools.sessions.visibility to 'all'. Gateway restarted. Confirmed working: sent 'James Bunny' from Telegram to Discord DM session. Also confirmed reverse: read Aaron's Discord message ('Test rabbit') from Telegram session via sessions_history. Cross-channel loop fully closed.

summary: Fixed cron delivery channel — both cron jobs updated to explicitly use Telegram
detail: Morning Brief and Daily Diagnostic cron jobs were using delivery.channel='last' which broke when Discord was added as a second channel. Error: 'Channel is required when multiple channels are configured: telegram, discord'. Fixed both jobs via 'openclaw cron edit --channel telegram'. Daily Diagnostic had errored at 6pm due to this issue — the job ran and produced a full report but failed to deliver.

summary: Configured Discord server channels — groupPolicy set to open, all 8 channels mapped
detail: Changed channels.discord.groupPolicy from 'allowlist' to 'open' to allow bot access to all server channels. Aaron @mentioned bot in all 8 Gustav-HQ channels to create sessions. Channel IDs saved to TOOLS.md: #general (1488499785131364605), #morning-brief (1488508798678077601), #diagnostics (1488508802650210425), #approvals (1488508811538071553), #social-content (1488508820052246638), #activity-log (1488508823718068355), #club-growth (1488508831268077598), #trading-desk (1488508835369844801). All 8 sessions confirmed active.

summary: Updated Morning Brief cron to mirror to Discord #morning-brief channel
detail: Aaron requested Telegram remain primary but Discord #morning-brief get a copy for archive purposes. Updated cron message to instruct: deliver to Aaron on Telegram, then post copy to Discord channel 1488508798678077601. Morning Brief cron ID: 657e7a64.

summary: Sent hype edit v6 to Discord #social-content channel
detail: Existing hype_v6 output (23s, 16MB) compressed to 4.5MB for Discord's 8MB bot limit using ffmpeg CRF 28. Sent to channel 1488508820052246638 (#social-content) via 'openclaw message send'. Message ID: 1488689966929154219. Aaron confirmed receipt.

summary: Built Agent Workspace live status page with real-time task display
detail: Created /workspace/mission-control/agent-workspace.html — standalone page with animated avatar card, live status indicator (idle/working/running/error), task description, activity bar, elapsed timer, and recent activity log. Status driven by agent-status.json (polled every 3s). Created agent-history.json for activity trail. Created scripts/agent-status.sh helper for updating status from tool calls. Status integrates with all future long-running tasks.

summary: Set up Tailscale for remote access to Agent Workspace from Aaron's phone
detail: Installed Tailscale via Homebrew. Aaron installed Tailscale on iPhone and created account (aaronstorey@me.com). Mac authenticated to Tailscale network via browser login URL. Both devices joined: Mac at 100.124.225.103, iPhone at 100.91.135.110. Web server started on port 18792 serving /workspace/mission-control/. LaunchAgent plist created at ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.gustav.workspace-server.plist for auto-start on boot. Permanent URL: http://100.124.225.103:18792/agent-workspace.html

summary: Added Infrastructure To-Do list and ROI Tracker to Mission Control
detail: Created todo.json with first task: set up elevated access for Gustav (passwordless sudo for specific commands). Added Infrastructure To-Do panel to Mission Control dashboard. Created roi.json tracking: hardware cost £1,200, monthly subscription £180, value returned £0, start date 2026-04-01. Added ROI Tracker card to Mission Control with live progress bar, dynamic total cost calculation, and value log. JavaScript loads roi.json and updates every 30s. Value entries logged with description, amount, date, category.

summary: Created Daily Income Research cron job — Opus 4-6, high reasoning, 4am daily
detail: Created cron job ID 7c5c35ff. Runs at 4:00am Europe/London daily. Model: anthropic/claude-opus-4-6 with thinking:high, 30-minute timeout. Process: reads previous ideas log → evaluates 5+ candidates → scores each (setup time, ongoing time, income potential, automation potential, risk) → picks best → researches thoroughly → appends to income-ideas-log.json → posts full report to Discord #trading-desk → posts 2-line summary to #morning-brief → updates todays-income-idea.json → logs token usage to usage-log.json. Research files created: income-ideas-log.json, usage-log.json, todays-income-idea.json.

summary: Updated Morning Brief cron to include daily income idea summary
detail: Updated morning brief message to read todays-income-idea.json — if updated today, includes 2-line income idea summary under '💡 Today's Income Idea' section. Keeps brief tight while surfacing the key idea each morning.

summary: Created Daily Activity Log Tidy cron job — 8pm daily, auto-logs the day's work
detail: Created cron job ID c27c27c1. Runs at 8:00pm Europe/London daily. Model: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, 10-minute timeout. Process: reads action-log.json for last ID → reads today's and yesterday's memory files → checks cron run logs → checks git log for commits → reviews usage-log.json → appends new entries to action-log.json for all significant work not already logged → posts summary to Discord #activity-log with key highlights. Will run first tonight at 8pm.

summary: Created Cloudflare account and tunnel for public workspace URL — superseded by Tailscale
detail: Aaron created Cloudflare account via Sign in with Apple. Installed cloudflared via Homebrew. Created API token (cfut_FOKm4mKrqtKUijfdgewOobO27fARlDqoAJEhd2Sb1ffe44b6). Account ID: 93b3b80e8ba8eefd290c90a4af2e1455. Created tunnel 'gustav-workspace' (ID: 9d62760c-bd36-4074-a98a-07d34768b635). Ultimately decided to use Tailscale instead — free, no domain required, already set up and working. Cloudflare account retained for future use (webhooks, static hosting, CDN). Status partial: tunnel created but not fully activated or used.

summary: Updated Mission Control Task Board to dynamically load from action-log.json
detail: Modified /workspace/mission-control/index.html — Task Board panel now fetches last 6 entries from action-log.json on page load and refreshes every 15 seconds. Previously the board was static HTML. Entries display with category badge, status indicator, and summary text. This means the dashboard automatically reflects new log entries without manual updates. Committed as part of the broader Mission Control update alongside ROI Tracker and Infrastructure To-Do panel.

summary: Morning brief cron fixed — wake mode, Discord delivery, Opus model, time moved to 10am
detail: Fixed 4 issues: (1) wake mode changed from 'now' to 'next-heartbeat' on all cron jobs to prevent gateway restarts triggering them. (2) Discord copy delivery fixed — cron isolated sessions don't have 'message' tool, switched to exec: openclaw message send instead. (3) claude-opus-4-6 was being rejected by cron (not in allowed models) — added to agents.defaults.models config. (4) Brief time moved from 9am to 10am to avoid Anthropic API peak congestion.

summary: Google Calendar connected — service account, recurring club classes and JKA events added
detail: Enabled Calendar API in Gustav Ops Google Cloud project. Created service account gustav-calendar@gustav-ops.iam.gserviceaccount.com, key saved to /workspace/credentials/google-calendar-service-account.json. Aaron shared aaronstorey007@gmail.com calendar with service account. Calendar script created at /workspace/scripts/check-calendar.py. Morning brief updated to include upcoming events. Added 11 recurring weekly club class events (red) and 12 JKA England national team events (orange) including European Championship (Norway, May), England Nationals (Crawley, Jun), and England Open (Crawley, Oct). England Nationals dual-coded as both club and national team event.

summary: Google Sheets member register built — 385 members, 12 monthly attendance tabs, dashboard
detail: Built full member register at https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-PuHqVEOvPnZsPM71oOe2KtjfWULDajs5tKin-2zsKc. Enabled Sheets and Drive APIs in Gustav Ops project. Imported 385 members from registration form by full name (not email) to capture siblings. Two-pass import found 54 missing siblings sharing emails. Priyanka Sharma added manually. 4 duplicates removed. Status column colour coded. Members before Oct 2022 marked inactive (11 exceptions). 12 monthly tabs Apr-26→Mar-27 with checkboxes, week colour coding, active members only, visitor section moved to far-right columns (immune to A-Z sorting). Dashboard with monthly financial breakdown, year totals, paying members chart. Expenses tab restructured into 12 monthly blocks.

summary: Designed Stratford Karate Club print-ready t-shirt artwork — black/gold, karate figure, spec sheet
detail: Built full print artwork for a sublimation t-shirt. Installed Inkscape via Homebrew for SVG→PNG rendering. Used Python + Pillow/NumPy for colour layer work. Sourced public domain karate silhouette (high kick) from Wikimedia Commons (OpenClipart, public domain). Recoloured figure gold by replacing fill in SVG source, rendered to transparent PNG, embedded as base64 in main SVG. Final design: 350mm × 450mm, black/gold colourway, geometric side panels, sunset circle emblem, club name + tagline. Output files: drafts/stratford-karate-design.svg (print artwork), drafts/stratford-karate-design-sheet.svg (supplier spec sheet with colour codes), drafts/SKC-tshirt-concept-v1.png (preview). Key lesson documented in memory: build geometry in Python SVG, embed public domain figures as base64, render via Inkscape CLI.

summary: Researched t-shirt suppliers, drafted quote emails, identified ArtMartialPrint as primary contact
detail: Researched martial arts sublimation printing suppliers. Used browser scrape skill to access JS-heavy ArtMartialPrint site — confirmed 6 karate products in their collection. Pricing: print-on-demand karate tees £25, hoodies £45. Identified SUB CLUB service for bespoke sublimation (60-100 units est. £12-18/shirt). Contact: info@artmartialprint.co.uk / Marco: 07870 195398. Secondary supplier: AM Custom Clothing (amcustomclothing.co.uk). Full quote brief drafted in drafts/tshirt-supplier-brief.md — ready to send once Gustav email account is active.

summary: Built Outlook account creation automation — completed all steps up to CAPTCHA, blocked by Arkose
detail: Attempted to automate creation of gustav.assistant@outlook.com via Playwright. Went through multiple iterations improving the script. Fixed playwright-stealth API (v2 uses 'from playwright_stealth import Stealth; Stealth().apply_stealth_sync(page)' — not old stealth_sync). Fixed Microsoft Fluent UI dropdown issue: label[for=BirthMonthDropdown] intercepts button clicks — added click_dropdown_label action to browser skill. Used fill_nth for name fields (ambiguous labels). Script create_outlook_account.py now handles steps 1-5 reliably: email fill → password → DOB (year, month, day) → name fields. Blocked at step 6 by Arkose 'Press and hold' CAPTCHA — cannot automate. Status: partial. Account requires Aaron to manually complete CAPTCHA. Suggested: aaron.storey.9 at outlook.com, password saved to Apple Notes as 'GUSTAV EMAIL - DO NOT SHARE'.

summary: Updated browser skill with click_dropdown_label action, stealth v2 fix, and Outlook signup script
detail: Added click_dropdown_label action to /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/browser/scripts/browser_agent.py — handles Microsoft Fluent UI dropdowns where label element intercepts clicks on the button. Fixed stealth import API to v2 syntax. Added create_outlook_account.py script to skills/browser/scripts/. Updated anti-bot-notes.md with Outlook account creation flow documentation (verified 2026-04-03). Committed as: 'Browser skill: add click_dropdown_label, Outlook signup script, stealth v2 fix — 03 Apr'.

summary: Income Research cron #2 ran — Fighter-Ready Digital S&C Programs identified as best opportunity
detail: Daily Income Research cron (ID: 7c5c35ff) ran at 04:00 BST. Model: claude-sonnet-4-6 (not Opus — cron model resolved to Sonnet). 5 candidates evaluated: Fighter-Ready S&C Programs (score 17/25 — WINNER), Lead Gen for Martial Arts Clubs (16), Print-on-Demand Merchandise (15), YouTube S&C Channel (14), Affiliate Marketing Content Site (13). Winner: sell downloadable karate-specific S&C programs on Payhip. Product suite: 3 one-off programs (£19–£67) + £9.99/month subscription. Market gap confirmed — only 1 real competitor found (Kumite Coach, £37, 0 reviews). Income projection: £350-700/month launch phase → £2,500-4,200/month by month 7-12. Aaron's time: 4-6 hours setup, ~1hr/quarter ongoing. Results appended to income-ideas-log.json, todays-income-idea.json updated, usage-log.json updated. Token usage: 275k input (88% cached), 12k output.

summary: Income Research cron #3 ran — Scaling After-School Karate Clubs via Sub-Instructors identified as best opportunity
detail: Daily Income Research cron (ID: 7c5c35ff) ran at 04:00 BST. Model: claude-sonnet-4-6. 92% cache hit (624k cached, 56k new input, 30k output), 17 tool calls. DuckDuckGo rate-limited after 5 queries (bot detection) — web search adapted. Idea: clone Aaron's existing school delivery model by hiring junior instructors (trusted brown/black belts at £15-20/hr) and deploying to 5-10 additional East London primary schools. 60+ primaries within 3 miles of E15 — Aaron already has the curriculum, the relationship model, and the instructor pool within his own club. Per-school economics: 18 kids × £6 = £108/session, £464/month revenue, £129 instructor cost, £335 profit (72% margin). Scaling: 3 schools → £1,005/month, 10 schools → £3,350/month. Academic-year annual at 10 schools: ~£33,500. Direct pipeline to club: 10 schools × 18 kids × 15-20% conversion = 27-36 new club members. Gustav automates: school outreach emails, pitch deck, contracts, parent sign-up forms, payment collection, scheduling, quality reporting. Results committed to income-ideas-log.json, todays-income-idea.json, usage-log.json (commit: 13c22e8). Mission Control updated.

summary: Income Research cron #4 ran — Karate Instructor Lesson Plan & Curriculum Subscription identified as best opportunity
detail: Daily Income Research cron (ID: 7c5c35ff) ran at 04:00 BST. Idea: B2B SaaS-style subscription for karate club owners and instructors — monthly lesson plan packs, curriculum frameworks, grading syllabi via Payhip. Price point: £19–29/month. 100 subscribers = ~£2,400/month recurring. Aaron outlines content quarterly (1-2hr/quarter), Gustav produces the PDFs and assets. Market gap: no dominant curriculum subscription product exists in the UK JKA space. Output committed to income-ideas-log.json, todays-income-idea.json, usage-log.json (commit: cf23c36).

summary: Fixed Activity Log cron timeout — dynamic file discovery, reduced timeout from 600s to 540s
detail: Root cause: cron prompt had hardcoded memory file paths (e.g. memory/2026-04-01.md). As days passed, referenced files grew large and the job consistently hit the 10-minute hard cap before completing delivery. Fix: updated prompt to use 'ls memory/ | sort | tail -3' to dynamically find today's and yesterday's files at runtime. Removed open-ended session transcript search from the process. Reduced job timeout from 600s to 540s to leave headroom for Discord delivery. Cron job ID: c27c27c1.

summary: Fixed income research cron to auto-commit output files to git after each run
detail: Income research cron (ID: 7c5c35ff) was writing results to research/*.json but not committing them to the workspace git repo. Files were being lost between diagnostic checks and dashboard refreshes. Fix: added step 11 to the cron prompt — 'git add research/ && git commit -m "chore: commit income research cron output [date]"'. Also manually committed today's already-written files (income-ideas-log.json, todays-income-idea.json, usage-log.json) as commit cf23c36.
